messages.author: Hextheplanet
messages.text: okay, following up on this now - I think in situations where you have lower numbers of hops, the path given by the firmware each way will generally be more likely to succeed especially when the links are strong. so cisien's success doesn't surprise me since I suspect he's going home -> home repeater -> mountain repeater and back
messages.content_html: okay, following up on this now - I think in situations where you have lower numbers of hops, the path given by the firmware each way will generally be more likely to succeed especially when the links are strong. so cisien's success doesn't surprise me since I suspect he's going home -> home repeater -> mountain repeater and back

messages.author: Hextheplanet
messages.text: some of the paths I see come through my repeater are really unfortunate, like a direct path that tries to hop from my repeater directly to jade's companion. from the local topology, that has very little chance of succeeding but it creates a lot of retries around me. so I know the path selection is out of your direct control but I am sure that's contributing to some
messages.content_html: some of the paths I see come through my repeater are really unfortunate, like a direct path that tries to hop from my repeater directly to jade's companion. from the local topology, that has very little chance of succeeding but it creates a lot of retries around me. so I know the path selection is out of your direct control but I am sure that's contributing to some

messages.author: Hextheplanet
messages.text: the broader question is, if it's so unreliable and meshcore-ha IS resetting the path appropriately, why keep landing on that same blackhole path?
messages.content_html: the broader question is, if it's so unreliable and meshcore-ha IS resetting the path appropriately, why keep landing on that same blackhole path?

messages.author: Hextheplanet

(edited)

messages.text: all of this not 100% confirmed because of course, there could be some sneaky other DE in the network, or whatever. but as best I understand what I describe above is what's happening, and we've done a few cycles of "turn it all on/off" that seem to indicate my assumptions about who is who in that packet exchange are correct

messages.author: Hextheplanet
messages.text: yeah, I am curious from the MeshCore firmware side what they see as the ideal state in the future for this stuff. I would assume it's that they take over more of the pathing micro, but given that IIRC the companion app right now manages it for DMs... might be worth it to confirm this
messages.content_html: yeah, I am curious from the MeshCore firmware side what they see as the ideal state in the future for this stuff. I would assume it's that they take over more of the pathing micro, but given that IIRC the companion app right now manages it for DMs... might be worth it to confirm this
messages.reply_html:
awol
definitely room for some sophistication there in pathing. I don't think you really know the quality of the path from the RX path log, but keeping a record of discovered paths and maybe a trace result or something could really improve it, but this also becomes a ton of state to manage and a hyper-optimization lol

messages.author: Hextheplanet
Hextheplanet
messages.text: from what I can see, all the traffic mostly started out as flood, so we got a lot of REQs to begin but as the backoff kicked in (since they were stacking up maybe*), HA traffic has since dropped. now, starting to see some of that flood routing shift over to direct, and starting to see a couple responses in my repeater that are direct. whether those will make it back to jade's companion I'm not sure.
messages.content_html: from what I can see, all the traffic mostly started out as flood, so we got a lot of REQs to begin but as the backoff kicked in (since they were stacking up maybe*), HA traffic has since dropped. now, starting to see some of that flood routing shift over to direct, and starting to see a couple responses in my repeater that are direct. whether those will make it back to jade's companion I'm not sure.

messages.author: Hextheplanet
messages.text: I think it's a hard question to answer. some of this is the protocol, and the path calculations are literally rerolling the dice right now and there's not much HA can do about that. from what I can tell, packets are making it back in your general direction, whether your companion is hearing them or not it doesn't seem to be showing up on your dashboard
messages.content_html: I think it's a hard question to answer. some of this is the protocol, and the path calculations are literally rerolling the dice right now and there's not much HA can do about that. from what I can tell, packets are making it back in your general direction, whether your companion is hearing them or not it doesn't seem to be showing up on your dashboard
